heading: Requirements for labeling certain toys and games
label: 1278 Requirements for labeling certain toys and games

L. 103–267, title I, § 101(e)108 Stat. 725June 16, 1994“(1).—In generalSubject to paragraph (2), a State or political subdivision of a State may not establish or enforce a requirement relating to cautionary labeling of small parts hazards or choking hazards in any toy, game, marble, small ball, or balloon intended or suitable for use by children unless such requirement is identical to a requirement established by amendments made by this section to the Federal Hazardous Substances Act [enacting this section] or by regulations promulgated by the Commission.“(2).—ExceptionA State or political subdivision of a State may, until , enforce a requirement described in paragraph (1) if such requirement was in effect on .”January 1, 1995October 2, 1993

children.heading: Toys or games for children who are at least 3
children.label: a Toys or games for children who are at least 3
A
children.text: is manufactured for sale, offered for sale, or distributed in commerce in the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: includes a small part, as defined by the Commission.

children.heading: Label
children.label: 2 Label
children.text: The cautionary statement required by paragraph (1) for a toy or game shall be as follows:

children.heading: Balloons, small balls, and marbles
children.label: b Balloons, small balls, and marbles
A
children.text: the packaging of such balloon, ball, marble, toy, or game,

children.label: B
children.text: any descriptive material which accompanies such balloon, ball, marble, toy, or game, and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of bulk sales of any such product when unpackaged, any bin, container for retail display, or vending machine from which such unpackaged balloon, ball, marble, toy, or game is dispensed,

children.heading: Balloons
children.label: A Balloons
children.text: In the case of balloons, or toys or games that contain latex balloons, the following cautionary statement applies:

children.heading: Balls
children.label: B Balls
children.text: In the case of balls, the following cautionary statement applies:

children.heading: Marbles
children.label: C Marbles
children.text: In the case of marbles, the following cautionary statement applies:

children.heading: Toys and games
children.label: D Toys and games
children.text: In the case of toys or games containing balls, the following cautionary statement applies:In the case of toys or games containing marbles, the following cautionary statement applies:

children.heading: Cautionary statement
children.label: A Cautionary statement
children.text: Any advertisement by a retailer, manufacturer, importer, distributor, or private labeler (including advertisements on Internet websites or in catalogues or other printed materials) that provides a direct means for the purchase or order of a product for which a cautionary statement is required under subsection (a) or (b) shall include the appropriate cautionary statement displayed on or immediately adjacent to that advertisement, as modified by regulations issued under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Requirement to inform
children.label: i Requirement to inform
children.text: A manufacturer, importer, distributor, or private labeler that provides such a product to a retailer shall inform the retailer of any cautionary statement requirement applicable to the product.

children.heading: Retailer’s requirement to inquire
children.label: ii Retailer’s requirement to inquire
children.text: A retailer is not in violation of subparagraph (A) if the retailer requested information from the manufacturer, importer, distributor, or private labeler as to whether the cautionary statement required by subparagraph (A) applies to the product that is the subject of the advertisement and the manufacturer, importer, distributor, or private labeler provided false information or did not provide such information.

children.label: i
children.text: in the primary language used in the advertisement;

children.label: ii
children.text: in conspicuous and legible type in contrast by typography, layout, or color with other material printed or displayed in such advertisement; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in a manner consistent with part 1500 of title 16, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.label: i
children.text: The terms “manufacturer”, “distributor”, and “private labeler” have the meaning given those terms in .section 2052 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: The term “retailer” has the meaning given that term in , but does not include an individual whose selling activity is intermittent and does not constitute a trade or business.section 2052 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to advertisements on Internet websites, 120 days after ; andAugust 14, 2008

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to catalogues and other printed materials, 180 days after .August 14, 2008

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: 3 Rulemaking
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of chapter 6 of title 5 or the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1980 ( et seq.), the Commission shall, not later than 90 days after , promulgate regulations to effectuate this section with respect to catalogues and other printed material. The Commission may, under such regulations, provide a grace period of no more than 180 days for catalogues and other printed material printed prior to the effective date of paragraph (1) during which time distribution of such catalogues and other printed material shall not be considered a violation of such paragraph. The Commission may promulgate regulations concerning the size and placement of the cautionary statement required by paragraph (1) of this subsection as appropriate relative to the size and placement of the advertisements in such catalogues and other printed material. The Commission shall promulgate regulations that clarify the applicability of these requirements to catalogues and other printed material distributed solely between businesses and not to individual consumers.44 U.S.C. 3501August 14, 2008

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 4 Enforcement
children.text: The requirements in paragraph (1) shall be treated as a consumer product safety standard promulgated under . The publication or distribution of any advertisement that is not in compliance with paragraph (1) shall be treated as a prohibited act under .section 2058 of this titlesection 2068(a)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: displayed in its entirety on the principal display panel of the product’s package, and on any descriptive material which accompanies the product, and, in the case of bulk sales of such product when unpackaged, on the bin, container for retail display of the product, and any vending machine from which the unpackaged product is dispensed, and

children.label: B
children.text: displayed in the English language in conspicuous and legible type in contrast by typography, layout, or color with other printed matter on such package, descriptive materials, bin, container, and vending machine, and in a manner consistent with part 1500 of title 16, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations thereto).

children.heading: Exception for products manufactured outside United States
children.label: 2 Exception for products manufactured outside United States
children.text: In the case of a product manufactured outside the United States and directly shipped from the manufacturer to the consumer by United States mail or other delivery service, the accompanying material inside the package of the product may fail to bear the required statement if other accompanying material shipped with the product bears such statement.

children.label: i
children.text: the package has a principal display panel of 15 square inches or less and the required statement is displayed in three or more languages; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the statement specified in subparagraph (B) is displayed on the principal display panel and is accompanied by an arrow or other indicator pointing toward the place on the package where the statement required by subsection (a) or (b) appears.

children.label: i
children.text: In the case of a product to which subsection (a), subsection (b)(2)(B), subsection (b)(2)(C), or subsection (b)(2)(D) applies, the statement specified by this subparagraph is as follows:

children.label: ii
children.text: In the case of a product to which subsection (b)(2)(A) applies, the statement specified by this subparagraph is as follows:

children.heading: Treatment as misbranded hazardous substance
children.label: e Treatment as misbranded hazardous substance
children.text: A balloon, ball, marble, toy, or game, that is not in compliance with the requirements of this subsection shall be considered a misbranded hazardous substance under .1section 1261(p) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “this section”.1
